notes: PRI 2010 §III.A. Per paper: all 50 states require lobbyists to register.

sub_component: A. Who is required to register
item_text: Public entities, other than government agencies.
Is there an exemption for government agencies and/or public officials acting within their official capacity?
scoring_guidance: REVERSE-SCORED per PRI 2010 footnote 85. Score 1 if NO such exemption exists; 0 if an exemption for government agencies/officials acting in official capacity does exist.
notes: PRI 2010 §III.B. Footnote 85 in source. Note: "exemption exists" reduces transparency; whether this counts positively or negatively in the score is not stated explicitly — flag for Phase 3.

item_text: Are government agencies relieved and/or exempted (partially) from the regulations imposed on non-government organizations and individuals?
scoring_guidance: REVERSE-SCORED per PRI 2010 footnote 86. Score 1 if government agencies are NOT relieved/exempted from regulations imposed on non-government orgs; 0 if they are.

item_text: Are government agencies subject to the same disclosure requirements as lobbyists?
Are government agencies subject to the same disclosure requirements as principals?
Does the law include a definition of "public entity"?
notes: PRI 2010 §III.C. Gate question — Table 5 reports C as 0/1 only, suggesting the published score reflected only this question. Sub-criteria C1–C3 below are described in the paper but their scoring contribution is not specified — flag for Phase 3.

item_text: Definition relies on Ownership.
notes: PRI 2010 §III.C. Sub-criterion of C0; only meaningful if C0=1.

item_text: Definition relies on Structure or composition of revenue.
notes: PRI 2010 §III.C. Sub-criterion of C0; only meaningful if C0=1.

item_text: Definition relies on Public charter or special government protection.
notes: PRI 2010 §III.C. Sub-criterion of C0; only meaningful if C0=1.

item_text: Does the law include a materiality test (ability to exempt) for coverage in disclosure regulations?
notes: PRI 2010 §III.D. Gate question — Table 5 reports D as 0/1 only.

item_text: Financial threshold exists: if expenses are less than some dollar threshold the entity is exempted from filing disclosure.
Dollar threshold value (USD) for financial-threshold exemption.
notes: PRI 2010 §III.D. Capture raw value; null if no threshold.

item_text: Time threshold exists: if amount of time devoted to lobbying is less than a threshold percentage of an individual's compensated time the individual or entity is exempted from filing disclosure.
Percentage threshold value for time-threshold exemption.
notes: PRI 2010 §III.D. Capture raw value; null if no threshold.

item_text: Are principals required to disclose?
notes: PRI 2010 §III.E1. Gate question for the rest of E1.

item_text: Are principals required to disclose their address and phone number?
Are principals required to disclose the names of all the lobbyists representing them?
Are principals required to disclose the address and phone number of contracted lobbyists?
Are principals required to disclose the nature of the business (public or private)?
Required component of disclosure report: Direct lobbying costs (compensation).
Required component of disclosure report: Indirect lobbying costs (non-compensation).
Required component of disclosure report: Other costs such as gifts, entertainment, transportation, and lodging.
Is the information disclosed in an itemized format (as opposed to an aggregated or lump-sum amount)?
Are principals required to disclose information on the issue lobbied by the general issues lobbied?
Are principals required to disclose information on the issue lobbied by the specific bill number or legislation ID?
Reporting frequency option: Monthly.
notes: PRI 2010 §III.E1.h. Capture each frequency option as a separate item; downstream scoring per PRI footnote 89 awards "1 point for reporting … less than three times a year" (paper wording is ambiguous) — defer scoring rule to Phase 3.

item_text: Reporting frequency option: Quarterly.
Reporting frequency option: Tri-annually (linked with legislative calendar).
Reporting frequency option: Semi-annually.
Reporting frequency option: Annually.
Reporting frequency option: Other (free-text).
Are principals required to disclose contacts?
Are principals required to disclose major financial contributors?
notes: PRI 2010 §III.E1.j. Per paper §III.E intro: scored independently of the rest of E (this is "question J" referenced in the text).
